mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 8.0% signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal or UNRATE fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and real sales (RSAFS) signals robust supply-side momentum that drives corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1) or 60-day returns underperformed baseline by > 15% in 3-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts (HOUST) below 450k units signals weak demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during recessions or HOUST fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes that raise equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite inflation spikes or CPIAUCSL shows no significant correlation with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances or INDPRO shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1) or 20-day returns underperformed baseline by > 10% in 3-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k jobs signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances or PAYEMS shows no significant correlation with returns (p > 0.1).
